fault_hypothesis: Lines 86-87 contain a spurious post-processing step that removes the last element when both streams end with the same value, incorrectly discarding a legitimate duplicate observation that must be preserved for accurate percentile calculations.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid inputs where duplicates must be preserved; it is always wrongly removing the last element.
After the merge loop and extend calls, the merged list must contain exactly as many elements as the sum of both input streams.
At each loop iteration end, the number of elements appended to merged equals the total number of elements consumed from both streams.
After the pop branch executes, the merged list length should still equal total inputs — but it won't because a valid duplicate was wrongly removed.
The returned merged list must contain all observations from both streams without any being dropped.
